nodes.title: Introduction to Aerodynamics
nodes.rationale: To prepare for a course, a beginner needs a solid understanding of what aerodynamics is and its fundamental importance in flight. This module sets the stage for all subsequent topics.
suggested_micro_topics: Key terms: air, fluid, flow, pressure, density

nodes.title: Fundamental Principles of Flight
nodes.rationale: Understanding how an aircraft stays in the air is crucial. This module covers the core forces that govern flight and how they interact.
suggested_micro_topics: Lift: generation and factors affecting it
Drag: types (form, skin friction, induced) and reduction

nodes.title: Airfoils and Wing Geometry
nodes.rationale: The shape of a wing (airfoil) is central to generating lift. This module explains the characteristics and terminology of airfoils and how they contribute to flight.
suggested_micro_topics: Airfoil nomenclature (chord, leading edge, trailing edge, camber)

nodes.title: Basic Fluid Dynamics Concepts for Aerodynamics
nodes.rationale: Aerodynamics is a branch of fluid dynamics. A basic grasp of how fluids (air) behave is essential to understand aerodynamic phenomena.

nodes.title: Introduction to Aerodynamic Testing
nodes.rationale: Understanding how aerodynamic theories are validated in practice is important. This module introduces basic experimental methods like wind tunnels.
suggested_micro_topics: Types of wind tunnels (subsonic, supersonic - conceptual)
Measurements in wind tunnels (lift, drag, pressure)
Introduction to CFD (Computational Fluid Dynamics) as a tool (conceptual)

raw_output:
```json
{
  "nodes": [
    {
      "node_id": "intro_aerodynamics",
      "title": "Introduction to Aerodynamics",
      "rationale": "To prepare for a course, a beginner needs a solid understanding of what aerodynamics is and its fundamental importance in flight. This module sets the stage for all subsequent topics.",
      "prerequisites": [],
      "suggested_micro_topics": [
        "Definition of Aerodynamics",
        "Historical overview of flight",
        "Branches of Aerodynamics (e.g., internal, external)",
        "Key terms: air, fluid, flow, pressure, density",
        "Importance in aerospace engineering"
      ]
    },
    {
      "node_id": "principles_of_flight",
      "title": "Fundamental Principles of Flight",
      "rationale": "Understanding how an aircraft stays in the air is crucial. This module covers the core forces that govern flight and how they interact.",
      "prerequisites": [
        "intro_aerodynamics"
      ],
      "suggested_micro_topics": [
        "Lift: generation and factors affecting it",
        "Drag: types (form, skin friction, induced) and reduction",
        "Thrust: how it's generated",
        "Weight: gravity's role",
        "Equilibrium of forces",
        "Bernoulli's Principle (simplified)",
        "Newton's Third Law in flight"
      ]
    },
    {
      "node_id": "airfoils_and_wings",
      "title": "Airfoils and Wing Geometry",
      "rationale": "The shape of a wing (airfoil) is central to generating lift. This module explains the characteristics and terminology of airfoils and how they contribute to flight.",
      "prerequisites": [
        "principles_of_flight"
      ],
      "suggested_micro_topics": [
        "Airfoil nomenclature (chord, leading edge, trailing edge, camber)",
        "Types of airfoils",
        "Angle of Attack (AoA)",
        "Stall phenomenon",
        "Wing planforms (rectangular, tapered, swept)",
        "Aspect ratio",
        "High-lift devices (flaps, slats - basic understanding)"
      ]
    },
    {
      "node_id": "basic_fluid_dynamics",
      "title": "Basic Fluid Dynamics Concepts for Aerodynamics",
      "rationale": "Aerodynamics is a branch of fluid dynamics. A basic grasp of how fluids (air) behave is essential to understand aerodynamic phenomena.",
      "prerequisites": [
        "intro_aerodynamics"
      ],
      "suggested_micro_topics": [
        "Properties of air (density, viscosity, compressibility)",
        "Laminar vs. Turbulent flow",
        "Boundary layers (basic concept)",
        "Continuity equation (simplified)",
        "Flow visualization techniques (conceptual)",
        "Introduction to Reynolds number"
      ]
    },
    {
      "node_id": "aerodynamic_testing_intro",
      "title": "Introduction to Aerodynamic Testing",
      "rationale": "Understanding how aerodynamic theories are validated in practice is important. This module introduces basic experimental methods like wind tunnels.",
      "prerequisites": [
        "principles_of_flight",
        "basic_fluid_dynamics"
      ],
      "suggested_micro_topics": [
        "Purpose of wind tunnels",
        "Types of wind tunnels (subsonic, supersonic - conceptual)",
        "Measurements in wind tunnels (lift, drag, pressure)",
        "Flow visualization in wind tunnels",
        "Scale models and similarity",
        "Introduction to CFD (Computational Fluid Dynamics) as a tool (conceptual)"
      ]
    }
  ]
}
```
